title: 60-Second Lead Response for Web Design Agencies
metaDescription: Every inbound web design lead replied to in under 60 seconds, in your studio's voice, with your booking link. We build the first one free on your real leads.
excerpt: Web design leads shop fast. The studio that replies first usually wins the discovery call. Set the SLA to under 60 seconds and let the system carry it.

sections.intro:
Web design and dev leads tend to inquire with several studios at once. The studio that responds first, with a real reply and a clear next step, almost always wins the discovery call. Waiting until the next morning to respond is the same as not responding.

The fix is mechanical. Every new lead gets a personalized reply in under 60 seconds, in your studio's voice, with your booking link. The team gets pinged at the same time so the right designer or PM can follow up by name. The lead never goes cold.

This is the Capabilities layer of an AI Operating System. We build it for you free, on your real leads, before you commit to anything else.
sections.fourCFit:
The 60-Second Lead Engine is the highest-leverage Capability for a studio because it captures the inquiry while interest is hot. It leans on the other three Cs:

  • Context: it writes in your studio's voice. We capture how you talk to clients so the reply sounds like you, not a template.
  • Connections: it lives on your form, inbox, and calendar. The lead lands in your CRM as the same record, never duplicated.
  • Capabilities: the reply itself, plus routing to the right account or PM based on project type.
  • Cadence: a no-show or no-reply triggers a follow-up sequence on day 1, day 3, and day 6 that stops the moment they reply.

It is the wedge into the larger system. See the full installable system at AI Operating System for Web Design Agencies.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
The SLA is concrete:

  • Reply lands in the prospect's inbox in under 60 seconds, every time.
  • Reply is personalized (their name, their company, the project type they mentioned), in your studio's voice.
  • Reply contains your booking link, restricted to slots you would actually take.
  • The right designer or PM gets pinged instantly with the lead and the reply that went out.
  • The lead is in your CRM as the same record.

If they do not book, a follow-up sequence runs on day 1, day 3, and day 6 in the same voice.
sections.howWeInstall:
Three phases. The Lead Engine is the one we offer to build free first:

  1. Audit, free, about 10 minutes. We score readiness across the Four Cs and confirm fit. Run the audit.
  2. Install the Lead Engine, free. We connect your form, inbox, calendar, and CRM. We capture your studio voice from past replies. First live reply usually goes out within a week.
  3. Operate, ongoing if you want us to. If the Lead Engine books calls you would have missed, we operate the rest of your AIOS on a monthly plan. See plans.
sections.expectedResults:
Lead response time has decades of data behind it. Studios that reply within minutes win materially more discovery calls than studios that reply within hours. The effect is large and consistent.

For a typical web design agency, here is what we plan against:

  • Median reply time: from hours to under 60 seconds.
  • Lead-to-discovery-call rate: a meaningful lift on the leads you were already getting.
  • Designer and PM time: lower, not higher. The system handles first reply and chase; humans only show up for the call.

Once you go past the free build, the AIOS engagement carries the standing guarantee: recover 40 or more hours a month, or we keep working free until you do.
sections.faq:
What does the free build include?

The full 60-Second Lead Engine wired into your form, inbox, calendar, and CRM, with your studio voice captured from past replies. No credit card, no time commitment.

What happens after the free build?

If it books calls you would have missed, we run the rest of your operations on a monthly plan. If it does not, we walk away and you keep what we built.

Who owns the system?

You do. Every prompt, connection, and automation lives in your accounts.
